title: Validate extracted URLs from DuckDuckGo scraper against SSRF guard before returning to agent
description:
SSRF-adjacent gap discovered during spec #529: OC src/web.rs search_duckduckgo (line 475) extracts URLs from the DuckDuckGo HTML response (scraper crate, CSS selectors .result__a[href]) and returns them to the caller without running validate_url on each extracted URL. A malicious or compromised DDG response could surface SSRF-interesting URLs (private IPs, metadata endpoints) into the search result list returned to the agent.

CC does not have this gap because CC's web_search delegates to the Anthropic API server tool; no URL extraction from third-party HTML is performed.

Validate_url (src/web.rs:153) is already implemented and handles all SSRF checks; it just is not called on extracted result URLs.

Done when: each extracted URL in search_duckduckgo is passed through validate_url before being added to results; URLs that fail validation are dropped (not surfaced to agent); a debug log line records dropped URLs.

Parent spec: #529
